ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh6-48 name field. National Pokedex no. 180. Johto Electric-type sheep mid-form.

trivia_bullets.text: Flaaffy (National Pokedex no. 180) represents a mid-evolution cost-of-power motif: as it stores more electricity in its wool, patches of fleece fall out, leaving skin exposed. Its skin compensates by becoming a rubber-like insulator to prevent self-discharge.

"It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches." One of the few Pokemon flavor texts that describes a visible physical consequence of the Pokemon's power increasing.

Flaaffy evolves into Ampharos at level 30. 'Ampharos' derives from 'ampere' (unit of electric current) + 'pharos' (Greek: lighthouse) — Ampharos canonically functions as a lighthouse beacon, illuminating sea lanes with its glowing tail. The pastoral sheep baseline gives way to a lighthouse keeper role at the endpoint.

Illustrated by sui, a recurring Pokemon TCG contributor whose work across multiple Sword and Shield sets tends toward warm, seasonal palette choices. The autumnal leaf-scatter setting for this Flaaffy is tonally consistent with sui's portfolio style.

related_cards.note: cross-print instances; corpus holds multiple Johto electric-sheep prints
